source_paper_title: Positivstellensätze and Moment Problems with Universal Quantifiers
context:
Page 26, Section 7 (Conclusions and Discussions)

Our work leads to many intriguing questions to explore in the future. For instance, without assuming archimedeanness, is there a preordering version of Theorem 3.4? Equivalently, does there exist a clean algebraic reformulation of the compactness (or emptiness) of the set \(K\) given with a universal quantifier as in (1.1)? Is there an analog of the Krivine-Stengle Positivstellensatz for such sets \(K\)?
title: Non-archimedean preordering (Krivine--Stengle type) Positivstellensatz for universally quantified sets
background_latex:
Let \(n,m,s\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\) and \(y=(y_1,\dots,y_m)\) be real variables. Let \(Q\subseteq\mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set and let \(\nu\) be a finite nonnegative Borel measure on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\), satisfying the multivariate Carleman condition
\[
\sum_{d=0}^{\infty}\Big(\int y_j^{2d}\,d\nu(y)\Big)^{-\frac{1}{2d}}=\infty,\qquad j=1,\dots,m.
\]
Let \(g=(g_1,\dots,g_s)\) be polynomials in \(\mathbb{R}[x,y]\). Define the universally quantified feasible set
\[
K:=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: g_j(x,y)\ge 0\ \forall y\in Q,\ j=1,\dots,s\}.
\]
For each subset \(I\subseteq\{1,\dots,s\}\), write \(g_I(x,y):=\prod_{j\in I} g_j(x,y)\) (with \(g_{\emptyset}(x,y):=1\)). Define the (integral) preordering cone associated with \((g,\nu)\) by
\[
\mathrm{PO}[g,\nu]:=\Big\{\sum_{I\subseteq\{1,\dots,s\}} \int \tau_I(x,y)\, g_I(x,y)\, d\nu(y)\ :\ \tau_I\in\Sigma^2[x,y]\Big\}\subseteq\mathbb{R}[x],
\]
where \(\Sigma^2[x,y]\) denotes the cone of sums-of-squares polynomials in \((x,y)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Problem: Show (or refute, by a counterexample) that for every polynomial \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) with \(f(x)>0\) for all \(x\in K\), one has \(f\in \mathrm{PO}[g,
u]\), without assuming any archimedeanness/compactness condition on \(\mathrm{PO}[g,
u]\) or on \(K\).

Equivalently, formulate and prove an appropriate Krivine--Stengle-type Positivstellensatz for \(K\) (e.g., an algebraic certificate in terms of \(\mathrm{PO}[g,
u]\) characterizing strict positivity, nonnegativity, emptiness of \(K\), and/or compactness of \(K\)).
latex:
Let \(n,m,s\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\) and \(y=(y_1,\dots,y_m)\) be real variables. Let \(Q\subseteq\mathbb{R}^m\) be a closed set and let \(\nu\) be a finite nonnegative Borel measure on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with \(\mathrm{supp}(\nu)=Q\), satisfying the multivariate Carleman condition

\[

Problem:
Problem: Show (or refute, by a counterexample) that for every polynomial \(f\in\mathbb{R}[x]\) with \(f(x)>0\) for all \(x\in K\), one has \(f\in \mathrm{PO}[g,\nu]\), without assuming any archimedeanness/compactness condition on \(\mathrm{PO}[g,\nu]\) or on \(K\).
